created_at.description: Date on which the pool was created. (RFC 3339 format)

updated_at.description: Date on which the pool was last updated. (RFC 3339 format)

values.ready: Pool has the right amount of nodes and is ready to process the workload
values.deleting: Pool is waiting to be processed for deletion
values.warning: Pool has some issues, check nodes
values.locked: Pool is locked because an abuse has been detected or reported
values.upgrading: Pool is upgrading its Kubernetes version

node_type.description: Node type is the type of Scaleway Instance wanted for the pool. Nodes with insufficient memory are not eligible (DEV1-S, PLAY2-PICO, STARDUST). 'external' is a special node type used to provision instances from other cloud providers in a Kosmos Cluster.

autoscaling.description: Defines whether the autoscaling feature is enabled for the pool.

size.description: Size (number of nodes) of the pool.

min_size.description: Defines the minimum size of the pool. Note that this field is only used when autoscaling is enabled on the pool.

max_size.description: Defines the maximum size of the pool. Note that this field is only used when autoscaling is enabled on the pool.

container_runtime.description: Customization of the container runtime is available for each pool.

autohealing.description: Defines whether the autohealing feature is enabled for the pool.

tags.description: Tags associated with the pool, see [managing tags](https://www.scaleway.com/en/docs/kubernetes/api-cli/managing-tags).

placement_group_id.description: Placement group ID in which all the nodes of the pool will be created, placement groups are limited to 20 instances.

kubelet_args.description: Kubelet arguments to be used by this pool. Note that this feature is experimental.

<kubelet_argKey>.description: Kubelet arguments to be used by this pool. Note that this feature is experimental.

zone.description: Zone in which the pool's nodes will be spawned.

root_volume_type.description:
Defines the system volume disk type. Several types of volume (`volume_type`) are provided:.
* `l_ssd` is a local block storage which means your system is stored locally on your node's hypervisor. This type is not available for all node types
* `sbs_5k` is a remote block storage which means your system is stored on a centralized and resilient cluster with 5k IOPS limits
* `sbs_15k` is a faster remote block storage which means your system is stored on a centralized and resilient cluster with 15k IOPS limits
* `b_ssd` is the legacy remote block storage which means your system is stored on a centralized and resilient cluster. Not available for new pools, use `sbs_5k` or `sbs_15k` instead.

values.l_ssd: Local Block Storage: your system is stored locally on your node hypervisor
values.b_ssd: Remote Block Storage: your system is stored on a centralized and resilient cluster (deprecated: will use sbs_5k instead)
values.sbs_5k: Remote Block Storage: your system is stored on a centralized and resilient cluster with up to 5k IOPS
values.sbs_15k: Remote Block Storage: your system is stored on a centralized and resilient cluster with up to 15k IOPS

public_ip_disabled.description: Defines if the public IP should be removed from Nodes. To use this feature, your Cluster must have an attached Private Network set up with a Public Gateway.

security_group_id.description: Security group ID in which all the nodes of the pool will be created. If unset, the pool will use default Kapsule security group in current zone.

labels.description: Kubernetes labels applied and reconciled on the nodes.

<labelKey>.description: Kubernetes labels applied and reconciled on the nodes.

taints.description: Kubernetes taints applied and reconciled on the nodes.

startup_taints.description: Kubernetes taints applied at node creation but not reconciled afterwards.
